steps.title: Identify favorable and total outcomes
steps.content: There are $45$ band members total, and $11$ of them play saxophone. The favorable outcomes are the saxophone players.

steps.title: Write the probability
steps.content: Probability is favorable outcomes over total outcomes: $$P(\text{saxophone})=\frac{11}{45}$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A, $\frac{1}{45}$, is one specific band member.
  • Choice B, $\frac{11}{45}$, uses the $11$ saxophone players out of $45$ members. Correct.
  • Choice C, $\frac{34}{45}$, is the probability of not selecting a saxophone player.
  • Choice D, $\frac{45}{45}$, would mean every band member plays saxophone.
That confirms B.
